constructors.name: Plane
constructors.summary: Constructs a default-initialized [Plane] with all components set to [code]0[/code].

constructors.name: Plane
constructors.summary: Constructs a [Plane] as a copy of the given [Plane].

constructors.name: Plane
constructors.signature: public Plane(float a, float b, float c, float d)
constructors.summary: Creates a plane from the four parameters. The three components of the resulting plane's [member normal] are [param a], [param b] and [param c], and the plane has a distance of [param d] from the origin.

constructors.name: Plane
constructors.summary: Creates a plane from the normal vector. The plane will intersect the origin. The [param normal] of the plane must be a unit vector.

constructors.name: Plane
constructors.summary: Creates a plane from the normal vector and the plane's distance from the origin. The [param normal] of the plane must be a unit vector.

constructors.name: Plane
constructors.summary: Creates a plane from the normal vector and a point on the plane. The [param normal] of the plane must be a unit vector.

constructors.name: Plane
constructors.signature: public Plane(Vector3 point1, Vector3 point2, Vector3 point3)
constructors.summary: Creates a plane from the three points, given in clockwise order.

members.name: DistanceTo
members.summary: Returns the shortest distance from the plane to the position [param point]. If the point is above the plane, the distance will be positive. If below, the distance will be negative.

members.name: GetCenter
members.summary: Returns the center of the plane.

members.name: HasPoint
members.signature: public bool HasPoint(Vector3 point, float tolerance)
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if [param point] is inside the plane. Comparison uses a custom minimum [param tolerance] threshold.

members.name: Intersect3
members.signature: public Variant Intersect3(Plane b, Plane c)
members.summary: Returns the intersection point of the three planes [param b], [param c] and this plane. If no intersection is found, [code]null[/code] is returned.

members.name: IntersectsRay
members.signature: public Variant IntersectsRay(Vector3 from, Vector3 dir)
members.summary: Returns the intersection point of a ray consisting of the position [param from] and the direction normal [param dir] with this plane. If no intersection is found, [code]null[/code] is returned.

members.name: IntersectsSegment
members.signature: public Variant IntersectsSegment(Vector3 from, Vector3 to)
members.summary: Returns the intersection point of a segment from position [param from] to position [param to] with this plane. If no intersection is found, [code]null[/code] is returned.

members.name: IsEqualApprox
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if this plane and [param to_plane] are approximately equal, by running [method @GlobalScope.is_equal_approx] on each component.

members.name: IsFinite
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if this plane is finite, by calling [method @GlobalScope.is_finite] on each component.

members.name: IsPointOver
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if [param point] is located above the plane.

members.name: Normalized
members.summary: Returns a copy of the plane, with normalized [member normal] (so it's a unit vector). Returns [code]Plane(0, 0, 0, 0)[/code] if [member normal] can't be normalized (it has zero length).

members.name: Project
members.summary: Returns the orthogonal projection of [param point] into a point in the plane.

members.name: D
members.summary: The distance from the origin to the plane, expressed in terms of [member normal] (according to its direction and magnitude). Actual absolute distance from the origin to the plane can be calculated as [code]abs(d) / normal.length()[/code] (if [member normal] has zero length then this [Plane] does not represent a valid plane). In the scalar equation of the plane [code]ax + by + cz = d[/code], this is [code skip-lint]d[/code], while the [code](a, b, c)[/code] coordinates are represented by the [member normal] property.

members.name: Normal
members.summary: The normal of the plane, typically a unit vector. Shouldn't be a zero vector as [Plane] with such [member normal] does not represent a valid plane. In the scalar equation of the plane [code]ax + by + cz = d[/code], this is the vector [code](a, b, c)[/code], where [code skip-lint]d[/code] is the [member d] property.

members.name: X
members.summary: The X component of the plane's [member normal] vector.

members.name: Y
members.summary: The Y component of the plane's [member normal] vector.

members.name: Z
members.summary: The Z component of the plane's [member normal] vector.

constants.name: PLANE_YZ
constants.summary: A plane that extends in the Y and Z axes (normal vector points +X).

constants.name: PLANE_XZ
constants.summary: A plane that extends in the X and Z axes (normal vector points +Y).

constants.name: PLANE_XY
constants.summary: A plane that extends in the X and Y axes (normal vector points +Z).

operators.name: operator !=
operators.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the planes are not equal. [b]Note:[/b] Due to floating-point precision errors, consider using [method is_equal_approx] instead, which is more reliable.

operators.name: operator *
operators.summary: Inversely transforms (multiplies) the [Plane] by the given [Transform3D] transformation matrix. [code]plane * transform[/code] is equivalent to [code]transform.affine_inverse() * plane[/code]. See [method Transform3D.affine_inverse].

operators.name: operator ==
operators.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the planes are exactly equal. [b]Note:[/b] Due to floating-point precision errors, consider using [method is_equal_approx] instead, which is more reliable.

operators.name: operator unary+
operators.summary: Returns the same value as if the [code]+[/code] was not there. Unary [code]+[/code] does nothing, but sometimes it can make your code more readable.

operators.name: operator unary-
operators.summary: Returns the negative value of the [Plane]. This is the same as writing [code]Plane(-p.normal, -p.d)[/code]. This operation flips the direction of the normal vector and also flips the distance value, resulting in a Plane that is in the same place, but facing the opposite direction.
